diagnosis: The player made steady progress through the first ~75% of the level (reaching x≈3400) but then fell at x=3318, y=420 and respawned at the start. After respawning, they spent ~10 seconds re-traversing early sections before stalling at x≈1130 for the final ~10 seconds of the session, making no further forward progress. The level is extremely wide (hundreds of tiles) with no mid-level checkpoints, so a single late fall forced a full re-traversal that the player did not complete. Enemy density and ladder clusters are present but do not create distinct spatial contexts; the layout repeats the same macro-pattern of long horizontal stretches punctuated by ladder climbs, leading to mechanical fatigue.

lessons.lesson: When a level's horizontal span exceeds 200 tiles without mid-level checkpoints, any fall in the latter half forces players to re-traverse 5+ minutes of content, converting challenge into tedium and causing disengagement.
conditions: no mid-level checkpoints or safe zones
falls occur in second half of level
lessons.outcome: player repeats 100+ seconds of traversal after each late fall, eventually quitting without completing
